intent: v1 file-watcher trio — replaces v0+ manual prompting with auto-trigger via fswatch. After this lands, T3 + T2 transition from interactive Claude/Codex to standby shell scripts that auto-process new task files. Junyan stops manually switching terminals. Meta-bootstrap: this is the LAST task that requires manual T3 prompting — once it's running, all future tasks flow auto.
context_files_required: docs/team/AGENT_ORCHESTRATION.md (§4 v1 草稿 — implementation reference, but NOT verbatim — actual implementation must verify cmd syntax)
bin/git-safe.sh (existing wrapper pattern to mirror style)
must_satisfy: TASK_A — bin/agent-watch-codex.sh (NEW, ~110 LOC bash)
A1. Shebang: `#!/usr/bin/env bash` on line 1
A2. `set -euo pipefail` on line 2

A3. Top-of-file docstring (15-25 lines) explaining: (1) what it does, (2) prerequisites (fswatch + codex CLI in PATH), (3) usage `bin/agent-watch-codex.sh`, (4) how to stop (Ctrl+C), (5) where logs go (stderr)
A4. Define constants near top: PENDING_DIR=.agent_tasks/pending, IN_PROGRESS_DIR=.agent_tasks/in_progress, DONE_DIR_BASE=.agent_tasks/done
A5. Pre-flight check 1: `cd` to repo root (use `git rev-parse --show-toplevel || pwd` fallback). If not in a git repo + no fallback works, exit 1 with clear message
A6. Pre-flight check 2: verify fswatch is installed (`command -v fswatch`); if missing print install instructions + exit 1
A7. Pre-flight check 3: verify codex command exists in PATH; if missing print install instructions + exit 1 (do NOT call it yet — just check)
A8. Pre-flight check 4: ensure PENDING_DIR + IN_PROGRESS_DIR exist (mkdir -p)
A9. Print startup banner to stderr: 'agent-watch-codex.sh standby [PID $$] — monitoring .agent_tasks/pending/'
A10. Trap SIGINT (Ctrl+C) to print 'Watcher stopped cleanly' before exit
TASK_A — main loop logic
A11. Use `fswatch -0 "$PENDING_DIR"` piped to `while read -r -d '' event` (NUL-separated for safety)
A12. On each event: list current pending/*.json files, pick the OLDEST one (`ls -1t -r ... | head -1`) — process FIFO order, not LIFO
A13. If no pending files, continue loop (idempotent under spurious events)
A14. Compute task_id = basename without .json extension
A15. Skip if `.agent_tasks/in_progress/$task_id.lock` exists (another watcher claimed it)
A16. Move task file to in_progress: `mv "$pending_file" "$IN_PROGRESS_DIR/"`
A17. Write lock file at $IN_PROGRESS_DIR/$task_id.lock with holder=codex + acquired_at + pid + expires_at (acquired_at + 30 min)
A18. Compute today's done dir: DONE_TODAY="$DONE_DIR_BASE/$(date -u +%Y-%m-%d)"; mkdir -p "$DONE_TODAY"
A19. Compute output path: OUT="$DONE_TODAY/$task_id.codex_output.json"

A20. Run codex headless with task file as input. Spec note: as of 2026-05, OpenAI Codex CLI supports `codex exec` for non-interactive one-shot. Try in this order (use first that works at codegen time, fallback chain):
      (a) `codex exec < "$IN_PROGRESS_DIR/$task_id.json" > "$OUT"`
      (b) `codex --input "$IN_PROGRESS_DIR/$task_id.json" --output "$OUT"`
      Write the WORKING command in the script + add comment showing the alternatives so Junyan can adjust if his codex CLI version differs

A21. Capture codex exit code in CODEX_EC. If non-zero: write a minimal failure-status JSON to $OUT (status: failed, agent: codex, error: 'codex exec exit $CODEX_EC') so T1 always sees an output even on failure
A22. Move task spec to done: `mv "$IN_PROGRESS_DIR/$task_id.json" "$DONE_TODAY/$task_id.research_task.json"`
A23. Remove lock: `rm -f "$IN_PROGRESS_DIR/$task_id.lock"`
A24. Log per-task summary to stderr: `[YYYY-MM-DDThh:mm:ssZ] codex processed $task_id (exit=$CODEX_EC) → $OUT`
A25. Continue main loop (do NOT exit after one task)
TASK_B — bin/agent-watch-reviewer.sh (NEW, ~110 LOC bash)
B1. Same shebang + set -euo pipefail + docstring pattern as TASK_A

B2. Constants: REVIEWS_GLOB='.night-shift/runs/*/reviews' — but fswatch needs concrete path; the script must dynamically iterate on first level (each run dir's reviews/) and watch each independently OR watch .night-shift/runs/ recursively
B3. Best approach: watch `.night-shift/runs/` recursively with fswatch (-r flag). Filter events to ONLY `*/reviews/*/READY` paths inside the loop
B4. Pre-flight checks: fswatch installed, claude command in PATH, .night-shift/runs/ exists
B5. Trap SIGINT clean exit
B6. Print banner: 'agent-watch-reviewer.sh standby [PID $$] — monitoring .night-shift/runs/*/reviews/*/READY'
TASK_B — reviewer main loop
B7. fswatch -0 -r ".night-shift/runs/" piped to while loop
B8. For each event: glob match `*/reviews/*/READY` files that are NEW (no corresponding code-review.txt yet)
B9. For each unprocessed READY: extract folder = $(dirname $READY_path)
B10. Skip if `$folder/code-review.txt` already exists (already processed)
B11. Skip if `$folder/code-review.tmp` exists (another watcher mid-write)
B12. Read request: REQUEST=$(cat $folder/code-review-request.txt)
B13. Run claude headless: `claude -p "..."` with prompt that includes the request text + instructions to follow REVIEWER_CHECKLIST.md + write verdict to stdout. Capture to $folder/code-review.tmp

B14. Suggested prompt template (Codex must implement, this is illustrative): `claude -p "You are T2 reviewer. Read REVIEWER_CHECKLIST.md and process this code-review-request.txt: $REQUEST. Write your verdict per the template at end of REVIEWER_CHECKLIST.md. Output format: VERDICT line first, then SUMMARY, CHECKLIST RESULTS, FINDINGS, TESTS_CHECKED, DEPLOYMENT_RISK, NOTES." > $folder/code-review.tmp`
B15. On claude exit 0: atomic rename `mv $folder/code-review.tmp $folder/code-review.txt`
B16. On claude non-zero: write a BLOCKED verdict to .tmp + rename to .txt + log error
B17. Log per-review summary to stderr: `[ts] reviewer processed $folder (exit=$EC)`
B18. Continue main loop
TASK_C — bin/agent-watch-setup.sh (NEW, ~50 LOC bash)
C1. Same shebang + set -euo pipefail + docstring pattern
C2. Step 1: detect OS (`uname -s`). If not Darwin (macOS), print warning that fswatch on Linux uses inotify-tools instead but proceed
C3. Step 2: check + install fswatch via brew (`brew list fswatch || brew install fswatch`). On Linux give equivalent apt-get instructions
C4. Step 3: chmod +x on all 3 watcher scripts (agent-watch-codex.sh + agent-watch-reviewer.sh + this setup script — defensive)

C5. Step 4: print colored (or formatted) startup instructions:
    Terminal 1 (T1 main): you keep using `claude --dangerously-skip-permissions` interactive
    Terminal 2 (T2 reviewer): run `bin/agent-watch-reviewer.sh` instead of interactive `claude`
    Terminal 3 (T3 codex): run `bin/agent-watch-codex.sh` instead of interactive `codex`
    Each watcher runs foreground; Ctrl+C to stop. Logs to stderr.
TASK_D — documentation updates

D1. Update docs/team/AGENT_ORCHESTRATION.md §4 'v1 — File Watcher (推荐先做这个)' subsection: replace the草稿 fswatch script with reference to bin/agent-watch-codex.sh. Same for reviewer. Add a paragraph: 'Implemented 2026-05-02 in commit <pending>. Junyan triggers via bin/agent-watch-setup.sh.'

D2. Update docs/team/AGENT_STARTUP_GUIDE.md: add new top-level section '## v1 Auto Mode (active 2026-05-02+)' BEFORE the existing v0+ sections. Document: (a) starting watchers via setup script, (b) what Junyan sees in each terminal, (c) how T1 still does spec-writing manually but T3+T2 are auto

D3. Update docs/team/CODEX_ONBOARDING.md: add note at top that 'In v1 mode, you do NOT run interactively — Junyan starts you via bin/agent-watch-codex.sh which calls codex exec headless. Same JSON schema applies.'
TASK_E — Hard prohibitions
E1. DO NOT modify bin/git-safe.sh or any existing bin/ script
E2. DO NOT modify .agent_tasks/ contents (the dirs are local-only state)
E3. DO NOT add new npm/pip dependencies
E4. DO NOT modify watchlist.json or any data files
E5. DO NOT modify any existing src/, scripts/, or api/ files
E6. DO NOT call codex or claude in this task's test_gate (unable to verify in T1 env; Junyan tests after script lands)
E7. DO NOT use bare `git` in any script — use `bin/git-safe.sh` if needed (but these watchers do NOT need git ops)
E8. DO NOT hardcode absolute paths — all paths must be relative to repo root (`git rev-parse --show-toplevel`)
E9. DO NOT remove SIGINT trap (Junyan must be able to Ctrl+C cleanly)
test_gate: cd /Users/years/Desktop/Stock/ar-platform && bash -n bin/agent-watch-codex.sh && echo 'codex-watcher syntax OK'
cd /Users/years/Desktop/Stock/ar-platform && bash -n bin/agent-watch-reviewer.sh && echo 'reviewer-watcher syntax OK'
cd /Users/years/Desktop/Stock/ar-platform && bash -n bin/agent-watch-setup.sh && echo 'setup syntax OK'
cd /Users/years/Desktop/Stock/ar-platform && [ -x bin/agent-watch-codex.sh ] && [ -x bin/agent-watch-reviewer.sh ] && [ -x bin/agent-watch-setup.sh ] && echo 'all 3 executable'

cd /Users/years/Desktop/Stock/ar-platform && grep -q 'set -euo pipefail' bin/agent-watch-codex.sh && grep -q 'set -euo pipefail' bin/agent-watch-reviewer.sh && grep -q 'set -euo pipefail' bin/agent-watch-setup.sh && echo 'safety mode set on all'
cd /Users/years/Desktop/Stock/ar-platform && grep -q 'fswatch' bin/agent-watch-codex.sh && grep -q 'fswatch' bin/agent-watch-reviewer.sh && echo 'fswatch referenced in both watchers'
cd /Users/years/Desktop/Stock/ar-platform && grep -q 'codex exec' bin/agent-watch-codex.sh && echo 'codex exec headless mode used'
cd /Users/years/Desktop/Stock/ar-platform && grep -q 'claude -p' bin/agent-watch-reviewer.sh && echo 'claude -p print mode used'
cd /Users/years/Desktop/Stock/ar-platform && grep -q '## v1 Auto Mode' docs/team/AGENT_STARTUP_GUIDE.md && echo 'v1 section in startup guide'
cd /Users/years/Desktop/Stock/ar-platform && grep -q 'agent-watch-codex.sh' docs/team/AGENT_ORCHESTRATION.md && echo 'orchestration doc references new script'

output_required.satisfies: per-bullet (A1-E9 = ~50 bullets) mark 'yes' or specific reason if 'no'
output_required.headless_command_chosen: exact codex command used (e.g. 'codex exec < $task_file > $OUT') + exact claude command used

production_task_purpose: Last manual-prompt task. After T1 commits + Junyan runs `bin/agent-watch-setup.sh` + restarts T2 and T3 terminals as watchers, future three-agent tasks flow without any manual terminal switching. T1 writes spec → T3 watcher picks up auto → T2 watcher reviews auto → T1 reads results.
